title: Trail Bot v1
summary: Autonomous hiking companion robot with computer vision and GPS navigation for trail monitoring and safety assistance.
detailsHtml:
Trail Bot v1 is an experimental autonomous robot designed to assist hikers and monitor trail conditions. Built with a Raspberry Pi 4 and equipped with computer vision capabilities, it can navigate simple terrain and provide real-time trail information.

Key Features

  • Autonomous navigation using GPS and computer vision
  • Real-time trail condition monitoring
  • Emergency communication capabilities
  • Weather-resistant design for outdoor use

Technical Implementation

The robot uses a combination of OpenCV for visual processing, GPS modules for positioning, and ultrasonic sensors for obstacle detection. The chassis is 3D-printed with weather-resistant materials and houses a 12V battery system for extended operation.

Current limitations include rough terrain navigation and battery life optimization, which are being addressed in the next iteration.

title: Smart Garden Monitor
summary: IoT sensor network for monitoring soil moisture, temperature, and light levels with automated watering system.
detailsHtml:
A comprehensive IoT solution for monitoring and maintaining garden health through automated sensing and irrigation. The system uses ESP32 microcontrollers networked together to create a distributed monitoring system.

System Architecture

Multiple sensor nodes collect data on soil moisture, ambient temperature, humidity, and light levels. Data is transmitted via WiFi to a central hub that processes the information and controls irrigation pumps as needed.

Features

  • Wireless sensor network with 6-month battery life
  • Web-based dashboard for monitoring and control
  • Automated irrigation based on plant-specific requirements
  • Historical data logging and analysis
  • Mobile notifications for critical conditions

The project has successfully maintained a vegetable garden for over 8 months with minimal manual intervention, resulting in healthier plants and 40% water savings compared to manual watering.

title: Climate Data Explorer
summary: Interactive web application for exploring historical climate data with D3.js visualizations and machine learning predictions.
detailsHtml:
An interactive web application that makes climate data accessible through compelling visualizations. The project combines historical weather data from NOAA with machine learning models to show trends and make future predictions.

Visualization Features

  • Time series charts showing temperature and precipitation trends
  • Geographic heat maps of climate anomalies
  • Interactive correlation matrices between climate variables
  • Animated transitions showing seasonal changes over decades

Machine Learning Component

The application includes a TensorFlow.js model trained on historical data to provide short-term climate predictions. The model shows accuracy rates of 85% for temperature predictions and 72% for precipitation forecasting over 30-day periods.

Built with D3.js for visualizations, Vue.js for the interface, and Python for data processing. The dataset includes over 150 years of climate records from weather stations across North America.

title: Sound-Reactive LED Installation
summary: Large-scale LED art installation that responds to music and ambient sound with real-time frequency analysis and pattern generation.
detailsHtml:
A 12-foot interactive LED installation that transforms sound into dynamic visual patterns. The system analyzes audio input in real-time and generates corresponding light patterns across 1,200 individually addressable LEDs.

Audio Processing

The installation uses Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) analysis to break down audio into frequency bands. Each band controls different aspects of the visualization:

  • Bass frequencies control color intensity and saturation
  • Mid-range frequencies drive pattern movement and speed
  • High frequencies influence brightness and sparkle effects

Hardware Implementation

Built with Teensy 4.1 microcontrollers for high-speed audio processing and LED control. The system includes:

  • Professional audio interface for clean signal input
  • Custom PCB designs for power distribution
  • Aluminum frame construction for durability
  • Modular LED strips for easy maintenance

The installation has been featured at three art galleries and music festivals, with consistently positive reception from both artists and audiences.

title: Gesture-Controlled Synthesizer
summary: Hand gesture recognition system for controlling software synthesizers using computer vision and MIDI output.
detailsHtml:
An innovative musical interface that translates hand gestures into MIDI control signals for software synthesizers. The system uses computer vision to track hand movements and finger positions, converting them into musical parameters.

Gesture Recognition

Using OpenCV and MediaPipe, the system tracks 21 hand landmarks in real-time at 60fps. Different gestures control various synthesizer parameters:

  • Hand height controls pitch and frequency
  • Finger spread adjusts filter cutoff
  • Hand rotation modifies resonance and effects
  • Pinch gestures trigger notes and control volume

MIDI Integration

The system outputs standard MIDI CC messages, making it compatible with any DAW or hardware synthesizer. Custom mapping allows users to assign gestures to specific parameters for personalized control schemes.

The interface provides a more intuitive and expressive way to control electronic music, particularly useful for live performance where traditional controllers may be limiting.

title: Perceptual Feedback in Running Biomechanics
summary: Investigation into how real-time perceptual feedback affects running form and injury prevention in recreational athletes.
detailsHtml:
This study examines the relationship between perceptual feedback mechanisms and biomechanical adaptations in recreational runners. Working with Brooks Running's research lab, we developed novel feedback systems to help runners optimize their form in real-time.

Research Methodology

Our approach combined motion capture analysis with haptic and auditory feedback systems to provide runners with immediate information about their gait patterns. We recruited 60 recreational runners and measured biomechanical changes over a 12-week training protocol.

Key Findings

  • Real-time feedback reduced ground contact time by 8.3% on average
  • Cadence improvements of 6-12 steps per minute observed across participants
  • Significant reduction in knee valgus angle during foot strike
  • Self-reported comfort and confidence increased by 23%

Clinical Implications

The findings suggest that perceptual feedback can be an effective tool for biomechanical intervention, particularly for injury prevention. The research contributes to our understanding of motor learning in running and has applications for both recreational and competitive athletes.

title: Multisensory Integration in Motor Learning
summary: Cross-modal research examining how visual, auditory, and haptic feedback combine to enhance skill acquisition in complex motor tasks.
detailsHtml:
This research investigates the mechanisms by which multiple sensory modalities contribute to motor skill learning and retention. Our work spans cognitive science, neuroscience, and applied psychology to understand optimal training protocols.

Experimental Design

We designed a series of controlled experiments using a custom haptic-visual interface to isolate the contributions of different sensory channels. Participants learned complex finger movement sequences under various feedback conditions.

Key Contributions

  • Demonstrated that haptic-visual coupling improves learning speed by 34%
  • Identified critical timing windows for multisensory integration
  • Developed predictive models for individual learning trajectories
  • Created open-source tools for multisensory experiment design

Applications

The findings have immediate applications in rehabilitation technology, sports training, and educational interfaces. Our work is being applied to develop next-generation training systems for surgical simulation and athletic performance enhancement.

title: Data-Driven Footwear Design Optimization
summary: Computational framework for personalizing running shoe design based on individual biomechanical signatures and performance metrics.
detailsHtml:
This collaborative research with Brooks Running develops machine learning approaches to optimize footwear design for individual runners. By combining biomechanical analysis with materials science, we're advancing personalized athletic equipment.

Methodology

Our framework integrates multiple data streams:

  • 3D gait analysis and force plate measurements
  • Pressure mapping and impact dynamics
  • Physiological monitoring during training
  • Longitudinal performance and injury tracking

Computational Innovation

We developed novel algorithms that predict optimal midsole geometry and material properties for individual runners. The system uses reinforcement learning to iteratively improve recommendations based on performance feedback.

Industry Impact

Our research has directly influenced Brooks' latest shoe designs, with beta testing showing 15% improvement in runner satisfaction and 12% reduction in impact-related discomfort. The work represents a significant step toward truly personalized athletic footwear.
